title: ranunculus ampelophyllus
intro: Ranunculus ampelophyllus, also known as the creeping ranunculus, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus ampelophyllus is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and may also be dried and processed into capsules or tinctures for medicinal use.
